copilot_dispatch_reason: Scope fully verifiable inline: 8 YAMLs + 2 MD files, all <1.2K LOC; yaml.safe_load + grep + AST walk sufficient to answer all 7 questions deterministically

assertions_verified.save_md_157_vs_handler_divergence: intentional — L157 is ANCHOR-audit subset, L2722 is runtime allow-list
assertions_verified.save_md_353_path_form_research: research/research.md (legacy basename also accepted, not incorrect)
assertions_verified.resume_confirm_has_no_parallel_line: pre-existing asymmetry, confirm uses different Q&A flow

findings.title: resource-map.md template lacks ANCHOR markers, making save.md:157 a no-op
findings.description: save.md:157 enumerates resource-map.md among docs requiring ANCHOR-tag validation, but the template itself has zero <!-- ANCHOR: --> markers today. The check trivially passes whenever resource-map.md is read. Fix by either (a) adding ANCHOR tags to the template or (b) dropping resource-map.md from the L157 enumeration.

findings.title: save.md:353 narrative omits description.json
findings.description: L353 enumerates indexed canonical docs but misses description.json, which IS re-scanned by the auto-index pass per SPEC_DOCUMENT_FILENAMES. Pure narrative drift; no runtime impact.

findings.title: save.md:353 uses path-form research/research.md vs Set basename research.md
findings.description: Narrative says research/research.md; source SPEC_DOCUMENT_FILENAMES Set uses bare research.md. Legacy research.md is also accepted at the root of a spec folder, so the path-form is not incorrect — just a stylistic divergence.

findings.title: resume_auto vs resume_confirm verbosity asymmetry (pre-existing)
findings.description: resume_auto.yaml:138 has an explicit 'Read supporting spec docs (tasks.md, checklist.md, plan.md, decision-record.md, resource-map.md)' activity line. resume_confirm.yaml has no parallel line (confirm uses a different Q&A + user-choice flow). The resource-map update was correctly applied to the line that exists. Not a packet 012 regression.

audit_b_resolution.claim: resume.yaml:57 glob should include resource-map.md for recovery scanning
audit_b_resolution.reasoning: Tier-4 glob is a session-detection mtime heuristic ranking active folders, NOT a context-recovery list. Adding resource-map.md would add noise: a one-time catalog edit would dominate tier-4 signal without indicating active work.

questions_answered.q1_all_8_yamls_valid_syntax: Yes — python3 yaml.safe_load passes on all 8; no duplicate keys
questions_answered.q2_resource_map_placement_consistent: Yes — after debug_delegation in all 6 YAMLs with a shared block
questions_answered.q3_save_md_157_matches_handler_error_str: Divergent by design — L157 is ANCHOR-audit subset (4 docs); L2722 is runtime allow-list (11 docs). Both internally correct.
questions_answered.q4_save_md_353_matches_filenames_set: Close but not exact — L353 omits description.json and uses research/research.md path-form (both minor)
questions_answered.q5_resume_yamls_consistent: Yes — order: and context_priority: strings match across auto/confirm. Auto has one extra verbose activity line (pre-existing asymmetry)
questions_answered.q6_resume_line_57_glob_miss: No — glob is session-detection heuristic, not recovery list. AUDIT-B possible-miss correctly rejected
questions_answered.q7_other_command_md_missed_updates: None — deep-research/deep-review use generic phrasing; create/agent and create/testing-playbook context-load enumerations don't require resource-map (optional doc)

recommendation: Ship packet 012 as-is. Optional <5 LOC polish: add ANCHOR tags to resource-map.md template (resolves W-001) OR drop it from save.md:157 enumeration.
